blurb: BlastDoor is a tightly sandboxed service that pre-processes all incoming iMessage content before it can reach the main Messages application process. All parsing of potentially untrusted data – including attachments, rich links, and message metadata – occurs within BlastDoor's restricted process, which has severely limited access to system resources, files, and network. Even if an attacker exploits a parsing vulnerability, the impact is contained within the BlastDoor sandbox. The system also implements exponential throttling of repeated failed exploitation attempts and re-randomizes ASLR layout between processing attempts, significantly raising the cost of zero-click exploit chains.
